questions.question: Who was the first President of independent India?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad served as the first President of India from 1950 to 1962.

questions.question: The Constitution of India came into force on:
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India was adopted on 26 Nov 1949 and came into force on 26 Jan 1950.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution abolishes untouchability?
questions.explanation: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice in any form.

questions.question: The Battle of Plassey was fought in the year:
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey (1757) established British dominance in Bengal after Clive defeated Siraj ud-Daulah.

questions.question: Who gave the slogan 'Jai Hind'?
questions.explanation: Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose popularized 'Jai Hind' as the national salute of the Indian National Army.

questions.question: The Rowlatt Act was passed in the year:
questions.explanation: The Rowlatt Act (1919) allowed detention without trial, which led to the Jallianwala Bagh massacre.

questions.question: Which river is known as the 'Sorrow of Bengal'?
questions.explanation: The Damodar River was known as the 'Sorrow of Bengal' due to frequent floods; now controlled by the DVC.

questions.question: Which is the largest state of India by area?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan is the largest state in India by area, covering approximately 342,239 sq km.

questions.question: The Tropic of Cancer passes through how many Indian states?
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer passes through 8 Indian states: Gujarat, Rajasthan, MP, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, WB, Tripura, Mizoram.

questions.question: Which is the smallest planet in our Solar System?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the smallest planet in the Solar System (Pluto is now classified as a dwarf planet).

questions.question: The chemical formula of table salt is:
questions.explanation: Table salt (sodium chloride) has the chemical formula NaCl.

questions.question: Vitamin C deficiency causes which disease?
questions.explanation: Scurvy is caused by deficiency of Vitamin C (ascorbic acid), leading to weak connective tissue.

questions.question: Which gas is known as 'Laughing Gas'?
questions.explanation: Nitrous oxide (N2O) is called laughing gas because it can induce laughter and euphoria.

questions.question: The Reserve Bank of India was established in:
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India was established on 1 April 1935 under the RBI Act, 1934.

questions.question: India's first Five Year Plan was launched in:
questions.explanation: India's First Five Year Plan was launched in 1951 with focus on agricultural development.

questions.question: GST in India was implemented from:
questions.explanation: GST (Goods and Services Tax) was implemented in India from 1 July 2017.

questions.question: Which country is the largest producer of coffee in the world?
questions.explanation: Brazil is the world's largest coffee producer, accounting for about 35% of global production.

questions.question: The headquarters of International Monetary Fund (IMF) is located in:
questions.explanation: IMF headquarters is in Washington D.C., USA, established in 1944 at the Bretton Woods Conference.

questions.question: Which vitamin is produced by the human body on exposure to sunlight?
questions.explanation: Vitamin D is synthesized in the skin when exposed to UV-B radiation from sunlight.

questions.question: Which planet is called the 'Red Planet'?
questions.explanation: Mars appears red due to iron oxide (rust) on its surface, earning it the nickname 'Red Planet'.

questions.question: The Fundamental Rights are enshrined in which Part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights are contained in Part III (Articles 12-35) of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Right to Education?
questions.explanation: Article 21A (inserted by 86th Amendment, 2002) provides free and compulsory education for children 6-14 years.

questions.question: How many High Courts are there in India?
questions.explanation: India currently has 25 High Courts.

questions.question: Who wrote the book 'Arthashastra'?
questions.explanation: Arthashastra is an ancient Indian treatise on statecraft, economic policy, and military strategy by Kautilya (Chanakya).

questions.question: The Quit India Movement was launched in:
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi on 8 August 1942, demanding immediate independence.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a greenhouse gas?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen (N2) constitutes ~78% of the atmosphere but is NOT a greenhouse gas; CO2, CH4, N2O are.

questions.question: The Sundarbans mangrove forest is located in:
questions.explanation: The Sundarbans, the world's largest mangrove delta, spans West Bengal (India) and Bangladesh.

questions.question: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately:
questions.explanation: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately 3 × 10^8 m/s (299,792,458 m/s).

questions.question: Which country won the FIFA World Cup 2022?
questions.explanation: Argentina won the FIFA World Cup 2022 held in Qatar, defeating France in the final.

questions.question: NITI Aayog replaced which body in 2015?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog (National Institution for Transforming India) replaced the Planning Commission on 1 January 2015.

questions.question: India's Parliament consists of how many houses?
questions.explanation: India's Parliament is bicameral — Lok Sabha (lower house) and Rajya Sabha (upper house), plus the President.

questions.question: The longest river in India is:
questions.explanation: The Ganga (2,525 km) is the longest river in India. The Indus is longer but most of it is outside India.

questions.question: The National Animal of India is:
questions.explanation: The Bengal Tiger was declared the National Animal of India in 1973 under Project Tiger.

questions.question: Who invented the telephone?
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell is credited with patenting the telephone in 1876.

questions.question: The United Nations was established in the year:
questions.explanation: The United Nations was established on 24 October 1945, after World War II.

questions.question: India's first satellite 'Aryabhata' was launched in:
questions.explanation: Aryabhata, India's first satellite, was launched on 19 April 1975 by a Soviet Kosmos-3M rocket.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor built the Taj Mahal?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Taj Mahal in Agra (1632–1653) as a mausoleum for his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are contained in which Part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSPs) are in Part IV (Articles 36-51) of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which is the most abundant element in the Earth's crust?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is the most abundant element in the Earth's crust, making up about 46% by mass.

questions.question: The Bhakra-Nangal dam is built on which river?
questions.explanation: Bhakra-Nangal dam is built on the Satluj River in Himachal Pradesh, one of India's largest dams.

questions.question: Chandrayan-3 successfully landed on the Moon's south pole in:
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3's Vikram lander made a successful soft landing on the Moon's south pole on 23 August 2023.
